dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic had the strongest positive HN vibe on balance: commenters mocked Claude Code’s massive leaked codebase, but the bigger discussion treated Claude’s exploit-writing and bug-finding ability as genuinely impressive [E1][E2].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic got two substantial front-page discussions, one mildly negative about Claude Code’s apparent bloat and one clearly impressed by Claude’s security capability. That gave it more meaningful positive coverage than OpenAI’s mostly mixed 'graveyard' thread, while Google Gemini and Microsoft Copilot only drew weak single-story mentions.

openai.judgementSnippet: Mixed. The big OpenAI thread framed the company as thrashy and overpromising, but top comments pushed back that frequent launches and shutdowns are normal experimentation; elsewhere, Codex got a small positive nod for its implementation quality [E3][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Slightly positive overall. HN criticized Claude Code’s huge leaked codebase as bloated and defensive, but a separate security thread treated Claude’s exploit-writing and bug-finding as impressive enough to outweigh that gripe [E1][E2].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Weak but positive. The only clear Gemini signal was a side comment describing it as useful for auto-tagging people and locations in digitized family videos [E5].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Weak and mildly negative. The only clear Copilot mention used it as shorthand for a quick POC that may 'work' but still lacks the rigor needed for production [E6].

evidence.summary: Story: 'Claude Code Unpacked : A visual guide.' Top comments focused on the leaked Claude Code codebase being around 500k lines, calling that excessive for an agent CLI and suggesting it reflected defensive complexity or AI-driven bloat.

Story: 'Claude wrote a full FreeBSD remote kernel RCE with root shell.' Comments emphasized that Claude could write the exploit from the CVE writeup, and one commenter added that Claude had also found the bug, producing an impressed tone about Anthropic capability.

Story: 'The OpenAI graveyard: All the deals and products that haven't happened.' The discussion mixed criticism of product thrash, staffing churn, and underdelivered voice mode with defenses that frequent launch-and-shutdown cycles are normal experimentation for a fast-growing startup.

Story: 'Show HN: Zerobox – Sandbox any command with file, network, credential controls.' A top comment praised the tool for building on a 'strong underlying library (the OpenAI Codex implementation),' giving OpenAI a small direct positive mention.

Story: 'Bring Back MiniDV with This Raspberry Pi FireWire Hat.' A commenter described a personal archival workflow that used Gemini to auto-tag family members and locations in old footage, presenting Gemini as useful in practice.

Story: 'The revenge of the data scientist.' One commenter referred to 'the POC someone slapped together in Copilot' as the kind of superficially working solution that still needs real evaluation before production, a mildly negative framing of Copilot output.
